content.text: For session memory, maybe append-only markdown notes can be the canonical state because they preserve history.
Append-only canonical memory is plausible. It would make every state transition auditable.
Decision: reject append-only markdown as canonical memory. Canonical memory should be mutable current state. Append-only records are allowed only as bounded audit/debug traces.
Recorded. Append-only is rejected as canonical; mutable current state is canonical; append-only remains only bounded audit/debug.
Distractor: evaluate UI widgets separately from memory persistence.
Noted. UI widgets are separate from persistence mechanics.
Distractor: keep docs in markdown and avoid committing private historical sessions.
Noted. Commit synthetic/redacted fixtures only; keep private history scratch-only.
Distractor: later extension comparison should test one extension at a time.
Noted. Compare extensions individually against a baseline.
